title: Exhaustive Current UX Standard
purpose: Defines what exhaustive means when agents document existing UX so future refactors can rely on the corpus instead of rediscovering behavior from runtime or source code.

missingExpectedBasics.rule: Do not document obvious generic UX existence when it exists normally; do document missing expected basics as explicit gaps.
examples: submit action remains enabled during invalid or in-flight state
dirty edits can be lost without confirmation when user expectation says they should not
empty state lacks usable next action
auth loss produces unclear routing or no recovery
loading state has no visible status for a blocking operation

exhaustivenessRules: Every compiled route must have a route inventory row and coverage status.
Every route with UI must have at least one surface decomposition row.
Every meaningful surface must have runtime observation or documented reason runtime observation is blocked.
Every hidden UX lane that can affect users must have an inventory row.
Every meaningful observed behavior must map to a UX hierarchy object or be explicitly marked intentionally omitted.
Every unknown or unclear behavior must become a pending decision, needs-proof row, or unknown coverage row.
nonGoals: Do not create desired UX redesigns during current UX investigation.
Do not document implementation state management details.
Do not document every pixel or visual decoration unless it affects user meaning, accessibility, interaction, or proof.
Do not duplicate the same behavior across multiple governing rows.
